title: Customer Communication Preferences and Marketing List

export_view.value:
The Marketing List enables you to leverage customer information to create targeted lists tailored to the products and services previously purchased by your customers. This valuable data enables you to efficiently send promotions, surveys, and review requests through various marketing software packages, enhancing your outreach and engagement efforts.

  • Increased Engagement - By targeting customers based on their purchase history, you can create personalized marketing campaigns that resonate more effectively, leading to higher engagement rates.

  • Streamlined Communication - Incorporating the Marketing List with other marketing software allows for seamless execution of campaigns, saving time and reducing the complexity of managing multiple platforms.

  • Better Insights and Analytics - Targeted lists provide valuable insights into customer preferences and behavior, helping you refine your marketing strategies and make data-driven decisions.

Collecting and acting on marketing consent helps you stay on the right side of global anti-spam and privacy laws. While the specific requirements vary by country, the underlying principle is consistent: you generally need clear, documented consent before sending promotional content to a customer, and you need to honor opt-outs promptly.

Depending on where your customers are located, some or all of the following may apply to you:

  • United States: The CAN-SPAM Act (email) and the TCPA (SMS and calls) set consent, disclosure, and opt-out requirements. State laws (such as the CCPA/CPRA in California) may add further obligations.

  • Canada: CASL requires express or implied consent for commercial electronic messages, with strict identification and unsubscribe requirements.

  • European Union and United Kingdom: The GDPR and ePrivacy Directive (PECR in the UK) require a lawful basis, typically opt-in consent, for direct marketing, along with clear disclosures and easy withdrawal of consent.

  • Australia: The Spam Act 2003 requires consent, sender identification, and a functional unsubscribe facility.

  • Other regions: Many other countries (Brazil's LGPD, Singapore's PDPA, and others) have comparable regimes.

The Email/SMS Marketing toggles described in this article helps you track consent, but they do not replace your own legal review. We strongly recommend working with qualified counsel in each jurisdiction where you operate to confirm your marketing practices are compliant.

Customer Marketing and Notification Settings

When managing a customer profile, you can customize your customer’s notification preferences for Service Email/SMS notifications and Marketing Email/SMS notifications. The customer’s SMS and Email information must be filled out for successful communication.

Note: Default selections for these preferences are managed in your Location Information details.

  • Receive Service Email Notifications - Authorize communication for Ticket Approvals and Automated Notifications.

  • Receive Email Marketing - When “Yes,” the customer’s email information is visible in the Marketing Report.

  • Receive Service SMS Notifications - Authorize communication for Ticket Approvals and Automated Notifications.

  • Receive SMS marketing - When “Yes,” the customer’s SMS information is visible in the Marketing Report.

Viewing the Marketing List

Depending on the user role, a link to the marketing list is on the RepairQ Dashboard. Under the Get Started section, click Target Marketing.

Alternatively, on the Navigation Bar, go to Reports. On the Reports page, click on the Marketing dropdown along the top row of report categories. From the available list, click Marketing List.

Marketing List

The marketing list provides customer details, including a link to their Customer Profile, communication preferences, and contact information. Results are based on the types of items and services that customers have previously purchased.

Marketing List Filters

You can use the available filters to scope results based on prior purchases/services from your customers.

Note: For successful results, you should select, at minimum, one item status, item type, and which customers to include in the report.

  • Date Range - Select a date range for your results. Leave this field blank to search based on all time.

  • Location - Select a specific location or run an aggregate report for multiple stores.

  • Item Status - Select the item status for your search. 

    • Pending - This will search for an item in Pulled status on a ticket.

    • Sold - This will search for an item in Sold status on a ticket.

  • Item Type - Select the item type(s) of products/services sold. Your results will include only the customers that purchased this item.

  • Catalog Item - If looking for customers who purchased a specific item, you can search by keyword, SKU, or UPC here.

  • Include customers who have purchased this item - Select this box if you want your search to include customers who purchased this item (Sale Item).

  • Include customers who have brought this item in for repair - Select this box if you want your search to include customers who brought this item in for repair (Repair Item).

  • Included customers who have opted out of marketing messages - Select this box if you want results to include those who have opted out of marketing messages.

Exporting the Marketing List

On the upper-right side of the page, you can click To Excel to export an Excel-friendly copy of the report, based on the filtered results.

START 1.21.X
